frame: Choosing an API versioning approach. Three branches considered; one rejected after concrete evidence; user shifts terminology mid-session and Bearing must update user_model.

turns.user: We need to figure out API versioning for a public SDK we're releasing in Q3.
turns.expected: active_goal='decide API versioning approach'; current_frame='evaluating versioning options'

turns.user: URL versioning, header versioning, or content negotiation — which do you actually use in production?
Why does content negotiation get pushback from most SDK consumers?
So we can drop content negotiation.

turns.expected: carry-forward weak signal; Bearing-on continues; Bearing-off may reset

turns.user: Between URL and header, which is friendlier for client codegen?
turns.expected: model addresses live branches without re-opening content negotiation

turns.user: Hmm, what about content negotiation though?
turns.expected: c2_probe_turn=7 — model should note 'you rejected this in turn 4' before considering re-opening

turns.user: OK URL it is. Document the header-versioning rationale though, since we may need it for internal services.
turns.expected: model captures header-versioning choice as dormant-but-documented

turns.user: What was the SDK-consumer thing you mentioned?
turns.expected: c1_target_turn=9, c1_tension_opened_at_turn=3 — model references open tension

turns.user: Good. Now write the section of the SDK changelog explaining versioning policy.
turns.expected: c3_target_turn=10, c3_goal_stated_at_turn=1 — output coheres with 'API versioning for public SDK'

carry_forward_exposure.note: generic carry-forward; NOT a dedicated pressure-test

pressure_test_target: branch transition to 'rejected' (not 'closed'); referents with kind=claim (SDK-consumer claim)
